channel.title: Peter Farrar Books — Blog
channel.description: Photography insights, leadership thinking, and updates on new books and projects.

item.title: Good Managers vs Bad Managers – The Behaviours That Shape Every Workplace
item.description: Managers have a disproportionate influence over motivation, trust, performance, and retention. It is not job titles or corporate slogans that shape culture — it is the small behaviours repeated every day.

item.title: From Top Performer to Top Leader – Why Great Employees Often Struggle as Managers
item.description: Excellent individual contributors do not automatically become effective leaders. Management requires a complete shift in identity.

item.title: ITIL 4 in Practice: What Companies Expect vs Real Service Management Leadership
item.description: Service management is far more dependent on leadership, culture, and execution than any framework alone. This gap is where most service desks succeed or fail.

item.title: The Zone System Explained – Part Two: Applying It in Modern Digital Photography
item.description: The tools have changed, but the thinking has not. How to apply the Zone System with histograms and RAW files in the digital era.

item.title: The Zone System Explained – Part One: Seeing Light Like Ansel Adams
item.description: At the centre of Ansel Adams' mastery was the Zone System — one of the most useful ways any photographer can learn to truly see a scene.

item.title: From $300 to $4,600: What Actually Scales a Photography Business
item.description: Most photography businesses don't fail because of a lack of talent. They fail because of weak positioning, poor pricing strategy, and inconsistent client experience.
